step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the consensus that rinsing the tooth with sterile saline helps maintain its viability while avoiding any risk of cellular damage through scrubbing. This approach aligns well with minimizing physical stress on the periodontal ligament and other essential structures needed for successful reimplantation. Therefore, my final answer remains **Option B**: rinse with saline and then attempt reimplantation. The gentle nature of salting preserves more of the tooth structure compared to mechanical scrubbing which might lead to irreparable tissue loss. Thus, Option B stands validated based on current dental practices aimed at maximizing outcomes post-reimplantation. My selected option stays consistent with earlier conclusions presented here. Final
